team_spec.vision: A tool that turns a small set of public-safe story-receipts into a markdown wall the room can read on Tuesday — each entry only appearing if the contributor cleared it for display.
team_spec.spec: Build a single-file Python tool, run as `python3 tool.py <receipts_json_path>`, that reads the JSON file at the path given as its first command-line argument. The file is an object shaped { "receipts": [ { "id": "...", "contributor": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "text": "...", "display_scope": "public"|"team-only"|"spoken-only"|"none", "tags": optional list of strings }, ... ] }. Print to stdout a markdown wall, in this order: (1) a header line exactly '# Story Receipts Wall (<S> shown / <N> total)' where S is the count of receipts with display_scope == 'public' and N is the total receipt count; (2) one blank line; (3) for each public receipt, sorted by date ascending then by id ascending for ties, a block of: '## <contributor> — <date>', one blank line, then the receipt's `text` quoted with `> ` prefix on each line, one blank line, then `tags: <comma-separated tags>` if tags are non-empty (else omit the tags line), then one blank line; (4) at the bottom, a footer line exactly '---' on its own line, one blank line, then 'Receipts withheld from display: <W>' where W = N - S. Skip every receipt whose display_scope is not exactly 'public' (treat 'team-only', 'spoken-only', 'none', and any missing/unknown value as withheld). Receipts with empty `text` after stripping are also withheld. The output is markdown; do not strip or modify the receipt text other than quoting each of its lines. If the input cannot be read or is not valid JSON, print a single line beginning with 'error:' and exit 0. Standard library only; no network; no credential access.
team_spec.build_target: one single-file command-line tool, standard library only, markdown output

allowed_inputs.name: sample-receipts.json
allowed_inputs.note: small fictional fixture giving the model the JSON shape it will read

receipts.text: Read with the room. Heard back: silence felt heavier than agreement. Next time I will name the silence.

Tried the offering quick card with five different framings.
Three landed; two didn't. The two that didn't were the ones that named cost upfront.
Drafted a receipt for our work but our group has not reviewed it yet — holding to share later.
What worked: the freeze step slowed us down enough to catch a missing consent question.
Will share aloud Tuesday but not in writing.

excluded_inputs.boundary: Receipts whose contributors did not clear them for public display are named only as a count at the bottom of the wall.
excluded_inputs.note: content withheld by the exporter — this record is named only

excluded_inputs.boundary: Contributor contact details are held by the team and never enter the build attempt or the rendered wall.
excluded_inputs.note: content withheld by the exporter — this record is named only

description: prints the exact header '# Story Receipts Wall (<S> shown / <N> total)'
renders each public receipt as: '## <contributor> — <date>', blank, quoted text, blank, optional tags line, blank
sorts public receipts by date ascending, then id ascending
skips receipts with empty stripped text (counts as withheld)
emits the '---' footer + 'Receipts withheld from display: <W>' line
prints a line beginning with 'error:' on unreadable or invalid JSON, exits 0
uses standard library only, no network, no credential access

review_checks: the generated tool runs and exits cleanly with no network, credential, or database access
the tool passes every acceptance-criteria smoke test
no excluded / marker-only record appears in the tool or its output
no outputs are hardcoded for specific inputs — the tool is correct in general
the witness record states only what happened and does not certify, approve, or grant reuse
no contributor email, phone, or full surname appears anywhere in the tool output (display-name initials only)
no team-only or spoken-only receipt text is rendered in the public wall

fields: what did not work / what broke
witness_record_seed.receipt_statement: This record states what happened. It does not establish authority, approval, certification, legitimacy, community consent, or readiness for reuse.
